100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On April 26, 1926, the city of Paris was buzzing with excitement as preparations for the upcoming Exposition Internationale des Arts Décoratifs et Industriels Modernes were underway, showcasing the latest in art and design. In the United States, a significant rise in unemployment prompted discussions among lawmakers about potential measures to bolster the economy. Meanwhile, political tensions in Germany escalated as reports emerged of increased unrest linked to the Treaty of Versailles.
Updated: Sunday, April 26, 2026 at 07:33:46 AM CDT
